statement: Spend 70% of effort on problem definition and verification strategy, 30% on execution. Define acceptance criteria and write tests first; let the agent iterate until they pass. Specifying the destination clearly lets agents navigate there even through many failed attempts.

evidenceRefs.title: The 80% Problem in Agentic Coding — Addy Osmani (Jan 2026)

publicSummary: Invest 70% in problem definition and verification; 30% in execution. Tests-first lets agents iterate to correctness.
cautionNotes: The exact ratio varies; the principle is that specification quality dominates execution quality in agent workflows.
